The revocation aligns with the Administrative Licensing Law and Securities Investment Fund Law, ensuring compliance in the financial sector.

full_content.analysis: The revocation of Jinzhou Bank Co., Ltd.'s public offering securities investment fund sales business license by the China Securities Regulatory Commission signifies a stringent regulatory environment for financial institutions. This action, grounded in the Administrative Licensing Law and Securities Investment Fund Law, underscores the importance of compliance within the banking and diversified financial services sectors. The implications of this decision may extend beyond Jinzhou Bank, potentially influencing investor confidence and operational practices across the industry as regulators continue to enforce standards aimed at maintaining market integrity and protecting investors.

title: Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau Issues Warning to Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd.
description: The Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau found Jianghai Securities' Harbin Hongjun Street department non-compliant with follow-up visit protocols and documentation. A warning letter was issued, requiring compliance improvements and a rectification report within 30 days.

key_points.details: Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd. faces compliance issues due to insufficient follow-up visits and incomplete documentation, violating regulatory measures.
The Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau has issued a warning letter, mandating the business department to enhance compliance and submit a rectification report.

full_content.analysis: The warning issued to Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd. underscores significant compliance failures in their operations, particularly regarding client follow-up and documentation. This action by the Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau not only mandates immediate rectification but also serves as a cautionary tale for other entities in the banking and diversified financial services sectors. The implications of this regulatory measure extend beyond Jianghai, as it reinforces the necessity for stringent adherence to compliance protocols across the industry, potentially reshaping operational standards and practices in the financial sector.

chinese_original:
江海证券有限公司哈尔滨红军街证券营业部：

经查，你营业部存在回访制度执行不到位、对个别客户回访不充分、留痕不完整的问题，违反了《证券公司和证券投资基金管理公司合规管理办法》（证监会令第166号）第三条、《关于加强证券经纪业务管理的规定》（证监会公告〔2010〕11号）第三条第四项的规定。

根据《证券公司和证券投资基金管理公司合规管理办法》第三十二条第一款、《关于加强证券经纪业务管理的规定》第八条的规定，我局决定对你营业部采取出具警示函的行政监管措施。你营业部应加强合规内控管理，并在收到本决定书之日起30日内向我局书面报告整改情况。

如果对本监督管理措施不服，可以在收到本决定书之日起60日内向中国证券监督管理委员会提出行政复议申请，也可以在收到本决定书之日起6个月内向有管辖权的人民法院提起诉讼。复议与诉讼期间，上述监督管理措施不停止执行。

黑龙江证监局

2025年12月26日
english_translation:
Jianghai Securities Co., Ltd. Harbin Hongjun Street Securities Business Department:

Upon investigation, it has been found that your business department has issues with the implementation of the follow-up visit system, insufficient follow-up visits for certain clients, and incomplete documentation, which violates Article 3 of the "Administrative Measures for Compliance Management of Securities Companies and Securities Investment Fund Management Companies" (CSRC Order No. 166) and Item 4 of Article 3 of the "Regulations on Strengthening the Management of Securities Brokerage Business" (CSRC Announcement [2010] No. 11).

In accordance with the provisions of the first paragraph of Article 32 of the "Administrative Measures for Compliance Management of Securities Companies and Securities Investment Fund Management Companies" and Article 8 of the "Regulations on Strengthening the Management of Securities Brokerage Business," our bureau has decided to impose an administrative regulatory measure of issuing a warning letter to your business department. Your business department should strengthen compliance and internal control management and submit a written report on rectification to our bureau within 30 days from the date of receipt of this decision.

If you disagree with this supervisory measure, you may apply for administrative reconsideration to the China Securities Regulatory Commission within 60 days from the date of receipt of this decision, or you may file a lawsuit with a competent people's court within 6 months from the date of receipt of this decision. The aforementioned supervisory measures shall remain in effect during the period of reconsideration and litigation.

Heilongjiang Securities Regulatory Bureau

December 26, 2025

impact_analysis.details: The warning letter emphasizes the need for banks involved in securities to enhance compliance, impacting their operational protocols.
This regulatory action highlights the importance of compliance in diversified financial services, potentially affecting their reputation and operational practices.

title: Jilin Securities Regulatory Bureau issues warning to Chongqing Kanghua Certified Public Accountants
description: The Jilin Securities Regulatory Bureau found multiple inadequacies in the audit procedures conducted by Chongqing Kanghua Certified Public Accountants for Jinyuan Environmental Protection Co., Ltd. and issued a warning letter requiring compliance with professional standards and internal management improvements.

key_points.details: The audit of Jinyuan Environmental Protection Co., Ltd. revealed significant deficiencies in cash, equity investments, and revenue procedures.
The Jilin Securities Regulatory Bureau issued a warning letter to the auditors for failing to comply with professional standards and regulations.
Auditors are mandated to enhance internal management and quality control systems to prevent future compliance failures.

full_content.analysis: The Jilin Securities Regulatory Bureau's warning letter to Chongqing Kanghua Certified Public Accountants highlights critical failures in the audit of Jinyuan Environmental Protection Co., Ltd. The identified deficiencies in audit procedures for cash, equity investments, and revenue raise concerns about the reliability of financial reporting. This regulatory action emphasizes the need for auditors to adhere strictly to professional standards and improve their internal controls. The implications of this decision extend beyond the immediate audit, as it serves as a reminder for all auditors to maintain rigorous compliance with established guidelines to uphold the integrity of financial disclosures. The requirement for a written report within twenty days further underscores the urgency for corrective measures.

chinese_original: 重庆康华会计师事务所（特殊普通合伙）及吴良辉、谢洪奇：经查，你们在金圆环保股份有限公司（以下简称金圆股份或公司）2024年度财务报表审计项目（康华表审（2025）A256号）执业中存在以下问题。一、货币资金审计程序执行不到位未获取公司个别发生额较大银行账户对账单，亦未对该账户本期发生额进行核验。未关注公司银行回函显示的异常情况，并进行核实。以上情况不符合《中国注册会计师鉴证业务基本准则》（2022年1月5日修订）第二十八条的规定。二、长期股权投资审计程序执行不到位对公司长期股权投资改为权益法核算的恰当性判断不充分，亦未获取被投资单位2024年度经审计财务报表，审计证据不充分。以上情况不符合《中国注册会计师鉴证业务基本准则》（2022年1月5日修订）第二十八条、《中国注册会计师审计准则第1301号——审计证据》（2022年12月22日修订）第十条的规定。三、在建工程审计程序执行不到位对在建工程暂估获取审计证据不充分，仅对28.53%的暂估金额获取审计证据。以上情况不符合《中国注册会计师审计准则第1301号——审计证据》（2022年12月22日修订）第十条的规定。四、应付账款审计程序执行不到位未对应付账款期初余额部分未回函供应商进行替代测试。对公司下属公司应付账款抽凭记录不完整，且未对异常情况保持合理的职业怀疑。对个别应付账款未回函供应商替代测试不到位。以上情况不符合《中国注册会计师审计准则第1331号——首次审计业务涉及的期初余额》（2022年12月22日修订）第八条，《中国注册会计师审计准则第1101号——注册会计师的总体目标和审计工作的基本要求》（2022年12月22日修订）第二十八条，《中国注册会计师审计准则第1312号——函证》（2010年11月1日修订）第十九条的规定。五、收入审计程序执行不到位未对公司下属公司过磅信息表中异常情况保持合理的职业怀疑。以上情况不符合《中国注册会计师审计准则第1101号——注册会计师的总体目标和审计工作的基本要求》（2022年12月22日修订）第二十八条的规定。你们上述行为违反了《上市公司信息披露管理办法》（证监会令第182号）第四十六条的规定。依据《上市公司信息披露管理办法》（证监会令第182号）第五十五条，我局决定对你们采取出具警示函的行政监管措施。你们应严格遵照相关法律法规和《中国注册会计师执业准则》的规定，及时采取措施加强内部管理，建立健全质量控制制度，确保审计执业质量；相关注册会计师应加强相关法律法规学习，勤勉尽责履行审计工作义务，防止类似情况再次发生。你们应当在收到本决定书之日起二十个工作日内向我局提交书面报告。如对本监督管理措施不服，可以在收到本决定书之日起60日内向中国证券监督管理委员会提出行政复议申请，也可以在收到本决定书之日起6个月内向有管辖权的人民法院提起诉讼。复议与诉讼期间，上述监督管理措施不停止执行。吉林证监局2026年1月6日
english_translation:
Chongqing Kanghua Certified Public Accountants (Special General Partnership), Wu Lianghui, and Xie Hongqi: Upon investigation, it has been found that during your audit of the financial statements of Jinyuan Environmental Protection Co., Ltd. (hereinafter referred to as Jinyuan or the Company) for the year 2024 (Kanghua Audit [2025] A256), the following issues were identified. 

1. The audit procedures for cash and cash equivalents were inadequately executed. You did not obtain bank reconciliation statements for significant individual bank accounts of the Company, nor did you verify the transactions for the current period in those accounts. You failed to pay attention to and verify the anomalies indicated in the bank confirmation letters received by the Company. These circumstances do not comply with Article 28 of the "Basic Standards for Assurance Services of Chinese Certified Public Accountants" (revised on January 5, 2022).

2. The audit procedures for long-term equity investments were inadequately executed. The judgment on the appropriateness of the Company's change to the equity method for accounting long-term equity investments was insufficient, and you did not obtain the audited financial statements of the investee for the year 2024, resulting in inadequate audit evidence. These circumstances do not comply with Article 28 of the "Basic Standards for Assurance Services of Chinese Certified Public Accountants" (revised on January 5, 2022) and Article 10 of the "Chinese Certified Public Accountants Auditing Standard No. 1301 - Audit Evidence" (revised on December 22, 2022).

3. The audit procedures for construction in progress were inadequately executed. The audit evidence obtained for the estimated amounts of construction in progress was insufficient, with audit evidence obtained for only 28.53% of the estimated amount. These circumstances do not comply with Article 10 of the "Chinese Certified Public Accountants Auditing Standard No. 1301 - Audit Evidence" (revised on December 22, 2022).

4. The audit procedures for accounts payable were inadequately executed. You did not perform alternative testing for the beginning balance of accounts payable for which supplier confirmations were not received. The documentation for accounts payable of the Company's subsidiaries was incomplete, and you did not maintain reasonable professional skepticism regarding anomalies. The alternative testing for certain accounts payable without supplier confirmations was inadequate. These circumstances do not comply with Article 8 of the "Chinese Certified Public Accountants Auditing Standard No. 1331 - Opening Balances Involved in Initial Audit Engagements" (revised on December 22, 2022), Article 28 of the "Chinese Certified Public Accountants Auditing Standard No. 1101 - Overall Objectives of the Auditor and Conduct of an Audit" (revised on December 22, 2022), and Article 19 of the "Chinese Certified Public Accountants Auditing Standard No. 1312 - Confirmation" (revised on November 1, 2010).

5. The audit procedures for revenue were inadequately executed. You did not maintain reasonable professional skepticism regarding anomalies in the weight information sheets of the Company's subsidiaries. These circumstances do not comply with Article 28 of the "Chinese Certified Public Accountants Auditing Standard No. 1101 - Overall Objectives of the Auditor and Conduct of an Audit" (revised on December 22, 2022).

Your aforementioned actions violate Article 46 of the "Administrative Measures for Information Disclosure of Listed Companies" (CSRC Order No. 182). In accordance with Article 55 of the "Administrative Measures for Information Disclosure of Listed Companies" (CSRC Order No. 182), our bureau has decided to take administrative regulatory measures by issuing a warning letter to you. You should strictly adhere to relevant laws and regulations and the provisions of the "Professional Standards for Chinese Certified Public Accountants," promptly take measures to strengthen internal management, establish and improve quality control systems, and ensure the quality of audit practice; relevant certified public accountants should enhance their study of relevant laws and regulations, diligently fulfill their audit obligations, and prevent similar situations from occurring again. You are required to submit a written report to our bureau within twenty working days from the date of receipt of this decision. If you are dissatisfied with this supervisory measure, you may apply for administrative reconsideration to the China Securities Regulatory Commission within 60 days from the date of receipt of this decision, or file a lawsuit in a competent people's court within six months from the date of receipt of this decision. The aforementioned supervisory measures shall remain in effect during the reconsideration and litigation period.

Jilin Securities Regulatory Bureau
January 6, 2026

chinese_original:
当地时间2026年1月8日，中共中央政治局委员、外交部长王毅在非盟总部同非盟委员会主席优素福举行中国—非盟第九次战略对话时表示，今年是中非开启外交关系70年，也是中国外长连续36年新年首访非洲，36年一以贯之原因何在？
首先是对中非友谊的传承。中非友谊源于我们肩并肩共同致力于民族独立与解放事业，又在各国国家的发展和经济建设中走向成熟。新年伊始，我们当然要像走亲戚那样先到非洲兄弟姐妹的家中做客。
二是体现对非政策的稳定。中国内外政策都保持着高度稳定性，给世界提供着宝贵确定性。对非交往也不例外。不管国际地区风云如何变幻，中国都将是非洲需要帮助时第一个向你们伸出援手的兄弟，也是在非洲需要寻找合作伙伴时第一个出现在你们身旁的朋友。
三是展现发展中国家的团结。中非同属全球南方，有着相似历史遭遇和共同理想追求。中非28亿人民团结奋斗，我们就能够战胜任何困难和挑战，加快中非共同走向现代化的步伐，也将推动这个世界更加公平合作、更加和谐稳定。
english_translation:
On January 8, 2026, Wang Yi, a member of the Political Bureau of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China and Minister of Foreign Affairs, held the ninth China-Africa Strategic Dialogue with Moussa Faki Mahamat, Chairperson of the African Union Commission, at the African Union headquarters. He stated that this year marks the 70th anniversary of the establishment of diplomatic relations between China and Africa, and it is also the 36th consecutive year that the Chinese Foreign Minister has made Africa the first stop of the New Year visit. What is the reason for this 36-year consistency?

First, it is the inheritance of China-Africa friendship. The friendship between China and Africa originates from our joint commitment to the cause of national independence and liberation, and has matured in the development and economic construction of our countries. At the beginning of the New Year, we certainly want to visit our African brothers and sisters as if we were visiting relatives.

Second, it reflects the stability of China's policy towards Africa. Both China's domestic and foreign policies maintain a high degree of stability, providing valuable certainty to the world. Engagement with Africa is no exception. Regardless of how the international and regional situation changes, China will always be the first to extend a helping hand when Africa needs assistance, and the first to appear by your side when Africa seeks partners for cooperation.

Third, it demonstrates the unity of developing countries. China and Africa are both part of the Global South, sharing similar historical experiences and common ideals. With the unity and hard work of 2.8 billion people in China and Africa, we can overcome any difficulties and challenges, accelerate our joint march towards modernization, and promote a more equitable, cooperative, harmonious, and stable world.

title: Chinese Special Envoy Zhai Jun Visits Israel for Bilateral Discussions
description: From January 6 to 8, 2026, Zhai Jun, the Chinese Special Envoy for Middle East Affairs, visited Israel to discuss bilateral relations with Israeli officials. Both sides emphasized the importance of maintaining healthy relations and cooperation.

key_points.details: Zhai Jun's visit underscores the commitment of both China and Israel to enhance bilateral relations, emphasizing mutual interests.
Israel's adherence to the One China policy highlights its diplomatic alignment with China, fostering deeper cooperation.
Discussions on regional hotspot issues indicate a shared interest in stability and collaboration in the Middle East.

full_content.analysis: The recent visit of Zhai Jun, China's Special Envoy for Middle East Affairs, to Israel marks a significant step in strengthening bilateral relations between the two nations. Both sides expressed a commitment to enhancing cooperation across various sectors, reflecting a mutual understanding of the importance of their long-standing friendship. Israel's adherence to the One China policy further solidifies its diplomatic stance, paving the way for deeper engagement with China. Additionally, the discussions on regional issues suggest a collaborative approach to addressing challenges in the Middle East, which could lead to increased stability and cooperation in the region. This visit not only reinforces the ties between China and Israel but also highlights their roles in regional diplomacy.

chinese_original:
2026年1月6日至8日，中国政府中东问题特使翟隽访问以色列，分别会见以色列外长萨尔、外交部政治总司长阿姆哈尼、国家安全委员会副主席德拉兹宁，就双边关系深入交换意见。
翟隽表示，中华民族和犹太民族传统友好，保持中以关系健康稳定发展符合两国人民根本利益。中方愿同以方共同努力，保持两国交往与互利合作，赓续人民友谊。
以方表示高度重视以中关系发展，始终奉行一个中国政策，愿同中方进一步密切两国各部门、各层级交往，推动双边各领域务实合作取得新进展。
双方还就地区热点问题交换了意见。
english_translation:
From January 6 to 8, 2026, Zhai Jun, the Special Envoy of the Chinese Government for Middle East Affairs, visited Israel, where he met separately with Israeli Foreign Minister Eli Cohen, Director General of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Alon Ushpiz, and Deputy Chairman of the National Security Council Eyal Hulata to conduct in-depth discussions on bilateral relations. 

Zhai Jun stated that the Chinese nation and the Jewish nation have a long-standing friendship, and maintaining healthy and stable development of China-Israel relations is in the fundamental interests of both peoples. The Chinese side is willing to work together with the Israeli side to maintain exchanges and mutually beneficial cooperation between the two countries and to continue the friendship between the peoples.

The Israeli side expressed high importance to the development of China-Israel relations, consistently adheres to the One China policy, and is willing to further strengthen exchanges at all levels and in all sectors with the Chinese side, promoting new progress in practical cooperation across various fields.

The two sides also exchanged views on regional hotspot issues.

title: Hong Kong Monetary Authority reopens 3-year Renminbi HKSAR Government Institutional Bonds for bidding
description: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority announced the reopening of RMB 1.25 billion of 3-year Renminbi HKSAR Government Institutional Bonds for bidding, available on January 15, 2026. The bonds will mature on July 28, 2028, with a 1.59% interest rate, and bidding is limited to primary market dealers.

key_points.details: The HKMA has reopened the 3-year Renminbi HKSAR Government Institutional Bonds for bidding, increasing the total issue amount by RMB 1.25 billion.
Bidding for the bonds will occur on January 15, 2026, with a semi-annual yield of 1.805% and a maturity date set for July 28, 2028.

full_content.analysis: The HKMA's announcement to reopen the 3-year Renminbi HKSAR Government Institutional Bonds signifies a strategic move to bolster infrastructure financing. By increasing the total issue amount by RMB 1.25 billion, the HKMA aims to attract primary market dealers, thereby enhancing liquidity in the bond market. The bonds, with a competitive interest rate of 1.59% per annum, will support infrastructure investments as outlined in the Infrastructure Bond Framework. This initiative not only provides a stable investment opportunity but also aligns with broader economic goals of improving infrastructure, which is crucial for sustainable growth in Hong Kong.

香港金融管理局（金管局）以香港特别行政区政府（特区政府）代表身分于今日（1月9日）公布，重开在基础建设债券计划下推出的3年期人民币特区政府机构债券（债券）（发行编号03GB2807001），作3年期债券供投标。该批重开债券将于2026年1月15日（星期四）进行投标，并于2026年1月19日（星期一）交收。

english_translation:
The Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA), on behalf of the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR Government), announced today (January 9) the reopening of the 3-year Renminbi HKSAR Government Institutional Bonds (Bonds) issued under the Infrastructure Bond Program (Issue Number 03GB2807001) for bidding. This batch of reopened bonds will be available for bidding on January 15, 2026 (Thursday) and will settle on January 19, 2026 (Monday). 

Through this reopening, an additional RMB 1.25 billion of the outstanding 3-year bonds (Issue Number 03GB2807001) will be available for bidding. These bonds will mature on July 28, 2028, with an interest rate of 1.59% per annum, paying interest semi-annually. The indicative price for this batch of bonds on January 9, 2026, is 99.47, with a semi-annual yield of 1.805%. 

Bidding is only open to primary market dealers appointed under the Infrastructure Bond Program. Any bid application must be submitted through a primary market dealer listed on the most recent published list, which can be downloaded from the Hong Kong Government Bond website at https://www.hkgb.gov.hk. Each bid must be in multiples of RMB 50,000 or its integer multiples. 

The bidding results will be announced on the HKMA website, the Hong Kong Government Bond website, Bloomberg (GBHK <GO>), and Refinitiv (IBPGSBPINDEX), with the announcement expected no later than 3:00 PM on the bidding day.

Hong Kong Monetary Authority  
January 9, 2026  

HKSAR Government Institutional Bond Bidding Information  
The bidding information for the 3-year Renminbi HKSAR Government Institutional Bonds is as follows:  
Issue Number: 03GB2807001  
Stock Code: 85039 (HKGB1.59 2807-R)  
Bidding Date and Time: January 15, 2026 (Thursday) from 9:30 AM to 10:30 AM  
Issue and Settlement Date: January 19, 2026 (Monday)  
Total Issue Amount: RMB 1.25 billion  
Term: 3 years  
Remaining Term: Approximately 2.52 years  
Maturity Date: July 28, 2028 (Friday)  
Interest: 1.59% per annum, paid semi-annually  
Interest Payment Dates: Annually on January 28 and July 28 from the issue date to the maturity date (inclusive), adjusted according to the terms specified in the memorandum of the Infrastructure Bond Program and the Government Sustainable Bond Program Institutional Bonds available on the Hong Kong Government Bond website.  
Bidding Form: Competitive bidding  
Bid Amount: Each competitive bid must be in multiples of RMB 50,000. Any bid application must be submitted through a primary market dealer listed on the most recent published list.  
Successful bidders on the bond issue date (January 19, 2026) must pay cumulative interest of RMB 381.16 for each minimum bid amount of RMB 50,000. (Due to rounding, the cumulative interest payable for bid amounts exceeding RMB 50,000 may slightly differ from the interest calculated based on the minimum bid amount of RMB 50,000).  
Other Details: Please refer to the memorandum available on the Hong Kong Government Bond website or consult a primary market dealer.  
Expected Start Date for Trading on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange Limited: The amount of bonds obtained from this bidding can be fully exchanged with the government bonds currently listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, stock code 85039 (i.e., Issue Number 03GB2807001).  
Use of Proceeds: The bonds will be issued under the institutional portion of the Infrastructure Bond Program. The proceeds will be used for infrastructure project investments as specified in the Infrastructure Bond Framework available on the Hong Kong Government Bond website.  

Hong Kong Monetary Authority  
January 9, 2026

impact_analysis.details: The reopening of these bonds under the Infrastructure Bond Program is expected to enhance funding for infrastructure projects, benefiting Infrastructure REITs.

title: Wang Yi and Ethiopian Foreign Minister Gedion Discuss Strategic Partnership
description: On January 8, 2026, Wang Yi met with Ethiopian Foreign Minister Gedion in Addis Ababa to strengthen China-Ethiopia cooperation and discuss mutual support in international affairs. They emphasized cultural exchanges and economic collaboration, including infrastructure projects and China's zero-tariff policy for African nations.

key_points.details: Wang Yi's visit underscores China's commitment to enhancing strategic cooperation with Ethiopia, emphasizing mutual benefits and cultural exchanges.
China's zero-tariff policy for African nations aims to bolster industrial cooperation and support Ethiopia's modernization efforts, benefiting the region.

full_content.analysis: The recent talks between China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi and Ethiopian Foreign Minister Gedion highlight the enduring strategic partnership between China and Ethiopia. This relationship is characterized by mutual respect and cooperation, particularly in the context of Africa's development. China's commitment to supporting Ethiopia through initiatives like the zero-tariff policy and infrastructure projects, such as the Addis Ababa-Djibouti Railway, reflects its broader strategy to strengthen ties with the Global South. The emphasis on cultural exchanges and people-to-people connections further solidifies this partnership, positioning both nations to collaboratively address regional challenges and promote modernization efforts. Overall, this engagement illustrates China's role as a key ally in Ethiopia's development journey.

chinese_original:
当地时间2026年1月8日，中共中央政治局委员、外交部长王毅在亚的斯亚贝巴同埃塞俄比亚外长格迪翁会谈。
王毅表示，中国外长新年首访非洲这个传统坚持了36年，风雨无阻，表明中方永远同非洲兄弟站在一起，致力于加强同广大发展中国家合作。此访首站选择埃塞，因为我们是全天候战略伙伴，应保持经常性战略沟通，加强全方位战略合作。中方赞赏埃塞将恪守一个中国原则的承诺落到实处，支持埃塞在多边机构享有更多话语权，愿同埃塞携手落实习近平主席提出的四大全球倡议，为中非关系发展继续发挥积极作用，在国际事务中维护全球南方利益，推动世界多极化进程。
王毅说，中国和埃塞关系建立在两国人民相知相亲基础上，希望双方利用“中非人文交流年”契机，不断深化民间交流，夯实两国友好民意基础。中方将继续从互利共赢和南南合作角度加强两国务实合作，共同推动亚吉铁路等项目焕发活力，为促进埃塞繁荣发展和地区互联互通发挥示范效应。双方要用好中方对所有非洲建交国实施零关税政策红利，拓展产业合作，共同走好现代化之路。
格迪翁表示，埃塞同中国的战略伙伴关系全方位、强有力。中国为埃塞发展提供了宝贵支持，体现两国友好的项目和故事在亚的斯亚贝巴无处不在。感谢中方在涉埃塞核心利益问题上给予有力支持，为非洲发展与和平提供宝贵帮助。习近平主席提出的中非共同推进现代化“十大伙伴行动”同非洲发展愿景完全契合，全球安全倡议为应对地区挑战发挥重要作用，中方对非洲国家零关税政策惠及非洲各国人民。这些都表明中国真正同全球南方站在一起。埃塞对此高度赞赏，愿继续同中方一道，推动双方全天候战略伙伴关系更好造福两国人民。
english_translation:
On January 8, 2026, local time, Wang Yi, a member of the Political Bureau of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China and Minister of Foreign Affairs, held talks with Ethiopian Foreign Minister Gedion in Addis Ababa. Wang Yi stated that the tradition of the Chinese Foreign Minister's first visit to Africa in the New Year has been upheld for 36 years, regardless of challenges, demonstrating that China will always stand with its African brothers and is committed to strengthening cooperation with developing countries. The choice of Ethiopia as the first stop for this visit is because we are all-weather strategic partners, and we should maintain regular strategic communication and enhance comprehensive strategic cooperation. China appreciates Ethiopia's commitment to upholding the One China principle and supports Ethiopia in gaining more voice in multilateral institutions. China is willing to work with Ethiopia to implement the four global initiatives proposed by President Xi Jinping, continue to play a positive role in the development of China-Africa relations, safeguard the interests of the Global South in international affairs, and promote the process of global multipolarity.

Wang Yi expressed that the relationship between China and Ethiopia is built on the mutual understanding and affection of the two peoples. He hopes that both sides will take advantage of the "China-Africa Year of Cultural Exchange" to continuously deepen people-to-people exchanges and solidify the foundation of friendship between the two countries. China will continue to strengthen practical cooperation between the two countries from the perspective of mutual benefit and South-South cooperation, jointly invigorating projects such as the Addis Ababa-Djibouti Railway, and demonstrating effects in promoting Ethiopia's prosperous development and regional connectivity. Both sides should make good use of the benefits of China's zero-tariff policy for all African countries that have established diplomatic relations, expand industrial cooperation, and jointly embark on the path of modernization.

Gedion stated that the strategic partnership between Ethiopia and China is comprehensive and robust. China has provided valuable support for Ethiopia's development, reflecting the friendly projects and stories of the two countries that are ubiquitous in Addis Ababa. He expressed gratitude for China's strong support on issues concerning Ethiopia's core interests and for providing valuable assistance for Africa's development and peace. The "Ten Partnership Actions" proposed by President Xi Jinping for jointly advancing modernization align perfectly with Africa's development vision, and the Global Security Initiative plays an important role in addressing regional challenges. China's zero-tariff policy for African countries benefits the peoples of all African nations. All of this demonstrates that China truly stands with the Global South. Ethiopia highly appreciates this and is willing to continue working with China to promote the all-weather strategic partnership to better benefit the peoples of both countries.

title: Zhai Jun Visits Palestine to Discuss China-Palestine Relations
description: On January 7, 2026, Zhai Jun, China's Special Envoy for Middle East Affairs, met with President Abbas in Ramallah to discuss bilateral relations and the Palestinian issue. Both sides emphasized the importance of their strategic partnership and cooperation, particularly in light of the upcoming China-Arab States Summit.

key_points.details: President Abbas emphasized the importance of the strategic partnership with China, highlighting its support for Palestine's just cause.
Both leaders discussed leveraging the ceasefire in Gaza to advance a political solution, underscoring the Palestinian Authority's role in governance.

full_content.analysis: The visit of Zhai Jun, China's Special Envoy for Middle East Affairs, to Palestine marks a significant step in strengthening China-Palestine relations. President Abbas's acknowledgment of China's support reflects a deepening strategic partnership, particularly in the context of the Palestinian issue and internal reconciliation efforts. The discussions around the ceasefire in Gaza and the potential for a political solution highlight the urgency of addressing long-standing conflicts. Furthermore, the Palestinian Authority's involvement in post-war governance signifies a proactive approach to regional stability. This engagement not only reinforces China's role as a key player in Middle Eastern diplomacy but also aligns with its broader objectives of fostering cooperation with Arab nations.

chinese_original:
2026年1月7日，中国政府中东问题特使翟隽访问巴勒斯坦。阿巴斯总统在拉马拉总统府会见翟隽，双方就中巴关系、巴勒斯坦问题等共同关心的议题交换了意见。
阿巴斯总统表示，巴中战略伙伴关系至关重要。巴方高度赞赏习近平主席和14亿友好的中国人民长期支持巴勒斯坦人民正义事业，赞赏中方推动巴勒斯坦内部和解的努力，将一如既往坚定支持中方核心关切，支持中国统一大业。巴方支持今年在华举办第二届中国一阿拉伯国家峰会，相信峰会的成功举办将进一步推动阿中合作。巴方希望以加沙停火为契机推动巴勒斯坦问题的政治解决，巴民族权力机构应有效参与加沙战后治理。
翟隽表示，中巴传统友好，在习近平主席和阿巴斯总统的关怀指导下，中巴关系实现跨越式发展。无论形势如何变化，中方都将同全世界爱好和平、支持正义的力量一道，坚定支持巴勒斯坦人民正义事业，为推动巴勒斯坦问题在“两国方案”基础上早日得到全面、公正、持久解决作出积极努力。
翟隽特使访巴期间还会见了巴外交部长法尔辛，就双边关系和共同关心的问题交换意见。
english_translation:
On January 7, 2026, Zhai Jun, the Special Envoy of the Chinese Government for Middle East Affairs, visited Palestine. President Abbas met with Zhai Jun at the presidential office in Ramallah, where both sides exchanged views on issues of mutual concern, including China-Palestine relations and the Palestinian issue. 

President Abbas stated that the strategic partnership between Palestine and China is crucial. The Palestinian side highly appreciates the long-term support of President Xi Jinping and the 1.4 billion friendly Chinese people for the just cause of the Palestinian people, and commends China's efforts to promote internal reconciliation within Palestine. The Palestinian side will continue to firmly support China's core concerns and the cause of national unification. The Palestinian side supports the holding of the second China-Arab States Summit in China this year and believes that the successful convening of the summit will further promote cooperation between Arab countries and China. The Palestinian side hopes to use the ceasefire in Gaza as an opportunity to advance a political solution to the Palestinian issue, and that the Palestinian National Authority should effectively participate in the post-war governance of Gaza.

Zhai Jun stated that the traditional friendship between China and Palestine has achieved leapfrog development under the care and guidance of President Xi Jinping and President Abbas. Regardless of how the situation changes, China will firmly support the just cause of the Palestinian people together with all peace-loving and justice-supporting forces around the world, and will make positive efforts to promote a comprehensive, just, and lasting resolution to the Palestinian issue based on the "two-state solution."

During his visit to Palestine, Special Envoy Zhai Jun also met with Palestinian Foreign Minister Riyad al-Maliki to exchange views on bilateral relations and issues of mutual concern.

title: Ministry of Finance Issues 2026 Book-Entry Interest-Bearing Treasury Bonds
description: The Ministry of Finance plans to issue 135 billion yuan in 1-year fixed-rate treasury bonds through competitive bidding on January 14, 2026. Interest will accrue from January 15, 2026, with payments due on January 15, 2027.

key_points.details: The Ministry of Finance plans to issue 135 billion yuan in 1-year fixed-rate treasury bonds, with interest starting January 15, 2026.
Competitive bidding for the treasury bonds will occur on January 14, 2026, using a modified multiple-price method to determine the coupon rate.
Distribution of the treasury bonds will occur from the end of bidding until January 15, 2026, with trading starting on January 19, 2026.

full_content.analysis: The issuance of 135 billion yuan in 1-year fixed-rate treasury bonds by the Ministry of Finance represents a significant move to manage government financing needs. The competitive bidding process scheduled for January 14, 2026, will determine the coupon rate, reflecting market conditions and investor sentiment. This issuance is crucial for maintaining liquidity in the financial markets and supporting government expenditure. The structured timeline for distribution and trading indicates a well-organized approach to bond issuance, which is essential for investor confidence and market stability.

chinese_original:
财办债〔2026〕4号
记账式国债承销团成员，中央国债登记结算有限责任公司、中国证券登记结算有限责任公司、中国外汇交易中心、上海证券交易所、深圳证券交易所、北京证券交易所：
财政部拟发行2026年记账式附息（一期）国债。现就本次发行工作有关事宜通知如下：

（一）品种。本期国债为1年期固定利率附息债。

title: National Development and Reform Commission selects investment consulting institutions
description: The National Development and Reform Commission of China has announced the selection of investment consulting and evaluation institutions through public bidding, effective February 1, 2026. This announcement abolishes a previous list of institutions.

key_points.details: The National Development and Reform Commission aims to enhance investment decision-making through standardized practices and selected consulting institutions.
The selection of investment consulting and evaluation institutions was conducted via public bidding, ensuring transparency and competitiveness.
This announcement aligns with existing regulations governing government investment and enterprise project approvals, reinforcing compliance.

full_content.analysis: The National Development and Reform Commission's announcement signifies a strategic move to improve the investment landscape in China. By selecting consulting and evaluation institutions through a public bidding process, the Commission aims to enhance the scientific rigor and standardization of investment decision-making. This initiative is crucial for ensuring that investments align with regulatory frameworks, thereby fostering a more transparent and efficient investment environment. The abolition of the previous list of institutions indicates a shift towards a more dynamic and responsive approach to investment consulting, which could lead to better project outcomes and increased investor confidence.

title: Shandong Regulatory Bureau Implements Central Committee's 15th Five-Year Plan Suggestions
description: On January 9, 2026, the Shandong Regulatory Bureau adopted the Central Committee's Suggestions for the 15th Five-Year Plan, focusing on fiscal policy, financial supervision, and coordination between government and market to enhance economic stability and development.

key_points.details: The Shandong Regulatory Bureau emphasizes the importance of aligning fiscal and monetary policies to enhance macroeconomic governance and support national strategies.
The bureau is committed to strengthening financial and accounting supervision, ensuring compliance and reliability of financial data to promote stability in financial institutions.
A proactive approach to risk monitoring and analysis is being adopted, particularly for small and medium-sized financial institutions, to safeguard financial stability.

full_content.analysis: The Shandong Regulatory Bureau's implementation of the 'Suggestions' from the Fourth Plenary Session of the 20th Central Committee is pivotal for aligning fiscal and financial policies with national development goals. By enhancing coordination between government and market forces, the bureau aims to ensure economic stability while addressing potential risks in financial operations. The focus on strengthening financial supervision and compliance will enhance the reliability of financial data, which is crucial for informed decision-making. This comprehensive approach not only supports the real economy but also promotes the effective use of fiscal resources, ultimately contributing to high-quality economic and social development in Shandong.

chinese_original:
2026年1月9日 来源：财政部山东监管局
党的二十届四中全会全面擘画“十五五”时期我国经济社会发展蓝图，会议通过的《中共中央关于制定国民经济和社会发展第十五个五年规划的建议》（以下简称《建议》）对发挥积极财政政策作用、加快建设金融强国等作出深刻论述并提出明确要求。山东监管局深入学习贯彻党的二十届四中全会精神，围绕财政部党组关于“加强、聚焦、优化”工作方针，锚定财政金融监管职责定位，坚持系统观念，统筹四个关系，有力推动中央财政金融政策落地见效，以高质量财政金融监管服务山东发展大局。
一、统筹财政与金融的关系，坚持凝聚合力，服务国家重大战略
《建议》指出，“强化国家发展规划战略导向作用，加强财政、货币政策协同”“增强宏观政策取向一致性”。工作中，山东监管局深刻认识“十五五”时期我国发展环境面临深刻复杂变化，牢牢把握财政和金融运行机制的协同和差异，持续聚焦中央财政金融政策执行，充分激发各类经营主体活力，提升宏观经济治理效能。不断强化财政金融政策协同，服务实体经济，助推产业升级，强化民生保障，推动稳经济、惠民生政策精准实施。强化中央政策性农业保险保费补贴资金监管，严厉查处虚假承保、虚假理赔等骗取财政补贴行为，确保各级财政资金花在刀刃上，守好国家粮食安全底线。聚焦设备更新改造贷款贴息、个人消费贷款贴息、服务业经营主体贷款贴息等政策落实中的难点堵点，及时协调多部门研究优化执行链条，简化办理程序，从供需两端发力提振消费，发挥财政资金的杠杆作用。围绕城市更新等重点工程以及新型政策性金融工具等重大政策，积极引导政策性银行服务国家重大战略，发挥好财政金融工具效能，提升经济金融适配性。
二、统筹政府与市场的关系，坚持底线思维，共同守牢安全底线
《建议》指出，“坚持有效市场和有为政府相结合”“坚持统筹发展和安全”。工作中，山东监管局依托财政金融监管的法定授权，把安全作为财政金融监管的永恒主题，强化底线思维和极限思维，着力提升监管工作的前瞻性、预判性和主动性。扎实开展属地中央金融企业风险监测分析和区域金融运行情况监测，每季度收集金融企业财务运行、资本运营、信贷投放等情况，通过重大事项报备和数据分析监测等方式，反映区域金融运行中的潜在问题和趋势变化，督促金融机构加强事前防范和事中整改。围绕推动中小金融机构改革化险、引导金融服务实体经济等重点，及时调研分析地方实践做法，有效识别、精准研判和及时预警存在的异常情况和共性问题。严格落实《财政部 金融监管总局关于在银行信贷等业务领域开展审计报告批量查验试点工作的通知》要求，使用财政部注册会计师行业统一监管平台核查审计报告真实性和合规性，规避金融机构在项目审批授信中面临的企业财务造假行为，为宏观政策的精准实施、优化资源统筹和资金调配提供坚实的数据基础，切实保障金融稳健运行，促进经济社会高质量发展。
三、统筹监督与服务的关系，坚持问题导向，持续加强财会监督
《建议》指出，“国家治理体系和治理能力现代化深入推进”，同时要“完善党和国家监督体系”“加强财会监督”。工作中，山东监管局深入贯彻落实中共中央办公厅、国务院办公厅《关于进一步加强财会监督工作的意见》，加强财会监督能力建设，加大追责力度，保障金融数据的真实可靠，提高监督精准度、震慑力、实效性。对重点金融机构实施会计信息质量全流程合规性监管，重点关注资产分类准确性、内控体系完备性等重点领域，对国有金融资本运行状况实施全级次穿透式监测分析，督促重点金融机构严格执行国家法定会计制度。加强中央金融企业驻鲁机构呆账核销、贷款减免、不良资产批量转让和收购处置等监管核查工作，重点关注制度的合规性、执行的充分性和程序的严谨性，特别是重点核查资产核销标准适用、批量资产定价机制等关键环节的合规性，护航金融机构合规稳健发展。聚焦金融机构融资供给端，通过严控信贷审批流程、强化资金流向全周期追踪。
四、统筹改革与发展的关系，坚持协同推进，优化联合监管机制
《建议》指出，“全面加强金融监管，强化央地监管协同，丰富风险处置资源和手段，构建风险防范化解体系，保障金融稳健运行”。工作中，山东监管局进一步优化监管部门间协调配合，完善与属地金融监管部门的常态化协同机制，加强上下联动、左右协同，各展所长，相向而行，握指成拳凝聚监管合力。综合运用多维度的监管手段，畅通政策传导渠道，扩大成果共享共用，增强政策取向的一致性。配合推进金融管理部门机构改革。扎实做好中央金融监管部门预算审核、预算执行监控、银行账户管理、资产管理等工作，深入研究部门预算管理、会计核算体系等体制深层次问题，持续关注机构改革过程中存在的难点问题。持续强化跨部门财政金融监管协同联动。统筹整合财政监管与金融监管的职能互补、资源共享和数据互通优势，探索同题共答，推动形成监管协同效应，有效提升联合监管效能。聚焦金融业态的前瞻性研究，围绕苗头性、倾向性问题开展联合调研，系统掌握重点领域政策执行效果，不断提升“草摇叶响知鹿过”的见微知著能力，做到有效识别、精准防控和妥善处置，促进相关长效治理体系完善发展。
english_translation:
On January 9, 2026, Source: Shandong Regulatory Bureau of the Ministry of Finance

The Fourth Plenary Session of the 20th Central Committee of the Communist Party of China comprehensively outlines the blueprint for China's economic and social development during the 14th Five-Year Plan period. The meeting adopted the "Suggestions of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China on Formulating the 15th Five-Year Plan for National Economic and Social Development" (hereinafter referred to as the "Suggestions"), which profoundly discusses the role of proactive fiscal policy and the acceleration of building a financial powerhouse, and puts forward clear requirements. The Shandong Regulatory Bureau deeply studies and implements the spirit of the Fourth Plenary Session of the 20th Central Committee, focusing on the Party Leadership Group of the Ministry of Finance's work guideline of "strengthening, focusing, and optimizing," firmly anchoring the responsibilities of fiscal and financial supervision, adhering to a systematic perspective, coordinating four relationships, and effectively promoting the implementation of central fiscal and financial policies to serve the overall development of Shandong with high-quality fiscal and financial supervision.

I. Coordinating the relationship between finance and financial services, uniting efforts to serve major national strategies

The "Suggestions" point out that "strengthening the strategic guiding role of national development planning, enhancing the coordination of fiscal and monetary policies," and "increasing the consistency of macro policy orientation." In practice, the Shandong Regulatory Bureau deeply recognizes the profound and complex changes in the development environment during the 14th Five-Year Plan period, firmly grasps the coordination and differences in the operation mechanisms of fiscal and financial systems, continuously focuses on the implementation of central fiscal and financial policies, fully stimulates the vitality of various business entities, and enhances the effectiveness of macroeconomic governance. It continuously strengthens the coordination of fiscal and financial policies, serves the real economy, promotes industrial upgrading, reinforces livelihood security, and drives the precise implementation of policies aimed at stabilizing the economy and benefiting people's livelihoods. It strengthens the supervision of central policy-based agricultural insurance premium subsidies, severely investigates fraudulent underwriting and claims to defraud fiscal subsidies, ensuring that fiscal funds at all levels are used effectively and safeguarding the bottom line of national food security. It focuses on the difficulties and bottlenecks in the implementation of policies such as interest subsidies for equipment renewal and transformation loans, personal consumption loans, and service industry operating entity loans, timely coordinating multiple departments to optimize the execution chain, simplify procedures, and stimulate consumption from both supply and demand sides, leveraging the role of fiscal funds.

II. Coordinating the relationship between government and market, adhering to bottom-line thinking, and jointly safeguarding safety

The "Suggestions" state that "we must adhere to the combination of an effective market and an active government" and "maintain a balance between development and safety." In practice, the Shandong Regulatory Bureau relies on its statutory authority in fiscal and financial supervision, making safety the eternal theme of fiscal and financial supervision, strengthening bottom-line thinking and extreme thinking, and striving to enhance the foresight, predictive ability, and proactivity of supervision work. It conducts risk monitoring and analysis of central financial enterprises in the region and monitors regional financial operations, collecting data on financial enterprises' financial operations, capital operations, and credit issuance quarterly. Through major event reporting and data analysis monitoring, it reflects potential problems and trends in regional financial operations, urging financial institutions to strengthen preemptive prevention and in-process rectification. It focuses on promoting the reform and risk mitigation of small and medium-sized financial institutions and guiding financial services for the real economy, timely researching and analyzing local practices, effectively identifying, accurately assessing, and promptly warning of existing anomalies and common issues. It strictly implements the requirements of the "Notice from the Ministry of Finance and the Financial Regulatory Administration on Conducting Pilot Batch Verification of Audit Reports in Banking Credit and Other Business Areas," using the unified regulatory platform for certified public accountants registered with the Ministry of Finance to verify the authenticity and compliance of audit reports, avoiding fraudulent financial behaviors in project approval and credit granting, providing a solid data foundation for the precise implementation of macro policies, optimizing resource allocation, and ensuring the stable operation of finance, thereby promoting high-quality economic and social development.

III. Coordinating the relationship between supervision and service, adhering to a problem-oriented approach, and continuously strengthening financial and accounting supervision

The "Suggestions" indicate that "the modernization of the national governance system and governance capacity is being advanced," while also emphasizing the need to "improve the supervision system of the Party and the state" and "strengthen financial and accounting supervision." In practice, the Shandong Regulatory Bureau thoroughly implements the "Opinions on Further Strengthening Financial and Accounting Supervision" issued by the General Office of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China and the General Office of the State Council, enhancing the capacity building of financial and accounting supervision, increasing accountability, ensuring the authenticity and reliability of financial data, and improving the precision, deterrence, and effectiveness of supervision. It implements full-process compliance supervision of accounting information quality for key financial institutions, focusing on the accuracy of asset classification and the completeness of internal control systems, and conducts comprehensive monitoring and analysis of the operational status of state-owned financial capital, urging key financial institutions to strictly adhere to national statutory accounting systems. It strengthens the regulatory verification of bad debt write-offs, loan reductions, and bulk transfer and disposal of non-performing assets for central financial enterprises in Shandong, paying special attention to the compliance of systems, the adequacy of execution, and the rigor of procedures, particularly focusing on the compliance of key links such as the applicability of asset write-off standards and bulk asset pricing mechanisms, ensuring the compliant and stable development of financial institutions. It focuses on the financing supply side of financial institutions, strictly controlling credit approval processes, and strengthening the tracking of fund flows throughout their lifecycle.

IV. Coordinating the relationship between reform and development, promoting synergy, and optimizing joint regulatory mechanisms

The "Suggestions" state that "we must comprehensively strengthen financial supervision, enhance the coordination between central and local regulatory bodies, enrich risk disposal resources and means, build a risk prevention and resolution system, and ensure the stable operation of finance." In practice, the Shandong Regulatory Bureau further optimizes coordination and cooperation among regulatory departments, improves the normalized collaborative mechanism with local financial regulatory bodies, strengthens vertical and horizontal coordination, leveraging each other's strengths and working together to form a unified regulatory force. It comprehensively employs multidimensional regulatory measures, smooths policy transmission channels, expands shared benefits, and enhances the consistency of policy orientation. It cooperates in promoting the institutional reform of financial management departments. It effectively carries out budget review, budget execution monitoring, bank account management, and asset management for central financial regulatory departments, deeply researching systemic issues in departmental budget management and accounting systems, and continuously paying attention to challenges during the institutional reform process. It continuously strengthens cross-departmental coordination in fiscal and financial supervision. It integrates the complementary functions of fiscal and financial supervision, shares resources, and facilitates data interoperability, exploring collaborative solutions to promote the formation of regulatory synergy and effectively enhance joint regulatory effectiveness. It focuses on forward-looking research in the financial sector, conducting joint research on emerging and trending issues, systematically grasping the implementation effects of policies in key areas, continuously improving the ability to detect subtle signs, effectively identifying, accurately preventing, and properly addressing issues, and promoting the improvement and development of relevant long-term governance systems.

title: Wang Yi and Moussa Faki Mahamat Hold Ninth Strategic Dialogue on China-Africa Relations
description: On January 8, 2026, Wang Yi and Moussa Faki Mahamat discussed enhancing China-Africa cooperation at the African Union headquarters, emphasizing mutual support and development. They highlighted the importance of the African Union in leading African unity and the significance of the Forum on China-Africa Cooperation.

key_points.details: The strategic dialogue emphasizes the importance of the African Union as a leader in fostering unity and cooperation among African nations, enhancing China-Africa relations.
China expresses commitment to support developing countries, particularly in Africa, to achieve modernization and uphold their rights in the global arena.
Both China and the African Union advocate for true multilateralism and a fair global governance system, reflecting their shared vision for international justice.

full_content.analysis: The recent strategic dialogue between China and the African Union marks a significant milestone in China-Africa relations, celebrating 70 years of diplomatic ties. This meeting underscores the African Union's pivotal role in promoting unity among African nations and enhancing cooperation with China. Both parties are committed to addressing global challenges through a collaborative approach, emphasizing the importance of multilateralism and the rights of developing countries. The dialogue reflects a mutual understanding of the need for a just global governance system, positioning both China and Africa as key players in the Global South. This partnership not only aims to foster economic development but also to uphold sovereignty and promote peace on the international stage.
